title: Buildah Static Make Target Appears broken
body:
When attempting to build a static binary of buildah using the `make static` target the build fails:

```
   > ok       cmd/link        13.741s
       > ok      cmd/link/internal/benchmark     0.070s
       > ok       cmd/link/internal/ld    5.216s
       > ok       cmd/link/internal/loader        0.144s
       > ok       cmd/nm  3.586s
       > ok       cmd/objdump     3.052s
       > ok       cmd/pack        2.179s
       > ok       cmd/trace       0.346s
       > ok       cmd/vet 10.014s
       > FAIL
       > go tool dist: Failed: exit status 1
       For full logs, run:
         nix log /nix/store/jb63gx4wwr661p3vbg2k6500haada84g-go-1.16.5.drv
error: 1 dependencies of derivation '/nix/store/f7qs32qpadydf3fzvps1sl64grs7aakq-buildah.drv' failed to build
make: *** [Makefile:72: static] Error 1
```

This appears to be related to the age of the nix environment and more specifically the version of go included therein.

It also appears that the `make static` target hasn't been tested as part of the CI suite for quite some time:
```
commit 8de75fd901f07ccf0abe624bf24402aa46a61f52
Author: Aditya Rajan <arajan@redhat.com>
Date:   2021-12-15 20:35:57 +0530

    .cirrus: remove static_build from ci
    
    Remove `static_build` from ci as it is becoming hard to keep up with
    `nix` hiccups in CI.
    
    ------
    Before Deciding to remove static_build from CI we tried things below :
    Things decided below worked but in order to keep maintaince simple
    consensus was made to remove static_build. Read more here:
    https://github.com/containers/buildah/pull/3679
    
    It seems nixos/nix:latest expects nixbld user to be added and specified in
    build-users-group but this should be only needed for multi-user
    mode.
    
    Hence I suspect latest push has a regression. Lock the nix to last working
    image.
    
    [NO NEW TESTS NEEDED]
    
    Signed-off-by: Aditya Rajan <arajan@redhat.com>
```

Is this still the proper mechanism for producing a static binary of buildah?

comments.body: Appears to be a symptom of https://github.com/golang/go/issues/71077

I don't think anyone from us is actually maintaining the nix package so I would say we should just remove it.

cc @nalind @flouthoc
We have a desire to build a staticly linked binary of buildah for inclusion in an initrd module so would like to see thst ability retained if possible.
I think nix support was added by @hswong3i , Afaik Official maintainers are not working on maintaining this.  It would be great if someone would like to contribute for this :)
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.

Doesn't nixos have a proper buildah package that could be used?
I don't see why upstream should carry these files here if nobody from the maintainers is actually using it so I would suggest we remove them. The files have not been maintained since almost 4 years https://github.com/containers/buildah/commit/2dee122f2aabc8374382649ceaba1f7363edd408 so it is using a very old nixos base.
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
